reasoning: The document exhibits significant content loss with a programmatic shingling recall of only 34.7%, which is corroborated by LLM findings of broken image assets and missing spatial data. Additionally, a major structural defect in the heading hierarchy (skipping H3) violates accessibility standards, making the document unfit for automated approval despite the pipeline's heuristic score.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy in the output skips level 3. It proceeds from level 2 ('June 25, 2025') directly to level 4 ('Parcel Updates:'). Level 5 ('Most recent Quarter') follows. According to the instructions, a skip from H2 to H4 is a defect as it breaks the logical structure required for screen reader navigation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The document contains a title, a date, and a legend header ('Parcel Updates:').
concerns.output_quote: The output contains an H2 (element index 1) followed by an H4 (element index 2).

concerns.analysis: The primary visual content of the source document—the color-coded map of North Carolina and the NCGICC logo—is broken in the HTML output. The screenshot shows broken image icons with alt text placeholders ('A map of North Carolina showing...' and 'NCGICC logo') instead of the rendered graphics. This results in the loss of all spatial data and branding from the visual output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains a central map of North Carolina counties and a NCGICC logo in the bottom right corner.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot displays two broken image icons with alt text 'A map of North Carolina showing the project status of parcel data by county, color-coded by the most recent quarter of the update.' and 'NCGICC logo'.
concerns.location: Main body of the document, following the 'Sources' section.

action_items: Investigate the cause of the 35% text recall to ensure all textual data was captured
Restore broken image assets for the map and agency logo
Correct heading levels to restore logical document structure for screen readers
